title: salix excelsa
intro: Salix excelsa, also known as the Dahu or White Willow, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix excelsa have been found to provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism. Salix excelsa is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
